g.text: A large republic makes the Union the remedy

g.title: cluster_389396bc
g.text: Factions arise unavoidably from human nature

g.title: cluster_6cf26583
g.text: If causes cannot be removed, only effects can be c

g.title: cluster_7f85133c

g.title: cluster_9bfe3003

g.title: cluster_d56fa29f
g.text: If democracy cannot cure faction, republic is pref

g.title: claim_0e5b4aa9
g.text: A pure democracy cannot provide a cure for the mischiefs of
faction because majorities can easily form, coordinate, and

g.title: claim_16dbfe9d
g.text: A large republic contains a greater variety of parties and
interests and makes it less likely and less feasible for a
majority faction to concert and execute oppressive schemes.

g.title: claim_2a26a834
g.text: The extent and proper structure of the Union provide the
best republican remedy for the diseases of faction.

g.title: claim_6aec36f6
g.text: Relief from faction can be sought only by controlling its
effects rather than by removing its causes.

g.title: claim_75baaa6a
g.text: A republic controls the effects of faction better than a
pure democracy because representation refines public views
and offers a cure unavailable in pure democracy.

g.title: claim_b47c122c
g.text: When a majority forms a faction in a popular government, it
can sacrifice the public good and the rights of other

g.title: claim_c53c3f32
g.text: The causes of faction are sown in human nature and cannot be
removed without unacceptable or impracticable measures.
